expectations.text: Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter
expectations.evidence: Main expression section shows it without quotes: 'FUNCTION = IMPACT(DZ(...), ...)'. The CMD block uses Adams solver (.adm) notation with integer marker IDs rather than Adams View CMD syntax.

notes: With-skill achieved 100% pass rate across all 20 assertions (8+6+6). Without-skill passed 17/20 (85%).

The 3 failed without-skill assertions: 'No adams_id specified' (simple-pendulum), 'length = 250' param name (spring-damper uses wrong name free_length), 'Expression wrapped in double quotes' (contact-force reverts to .adm syntax).
The adams_id failure is the most significant — the baseline model consistently adds explicit IDs which is error-prone. The skill explicitly prohibits this.
The 'length' vs 'free_length' failure shows the baseline model guesses a descriptive parameter name rather than the actual Adams CMD keyword.
The FUNCTION= quoting failure shows the baseline model reverts to Adams solver .adm dataset notation rather than Adams View CMD syntax.
Timing/token data was not captured for these runs.

Compared to iteration-1 (100% with-skill, 87% without-skill), results are consistent. The small delta (+15%) reflects that these evals test relatively foundational Adams CMD patterns. Evals targeting more specialized topics (custom forces, parameterization, loops) would show larger deltas.
